task_completion.description: Open the correct w1 (cell body) and w2 (nucleus) image pair.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji interface showing two open image windows with filenames containing 'C1' and ending in 'w1' and 'w2' (or similar TIF extensions).

task_completion.description: Set the image spatial scale to 0.65 microns per pixel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji 'Set Scale' dialog visible with 'Distance in pixels' = 1, 'Known distance' = 0.65, and 'Unit of length' = um/micron, OR the image window header displaying physical dimensions (um) instead of pixels.

task_completion.description: Create a composite image with the cell body in green and nucleus in red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A merged color image window in Fiji where the larger cell body area is tinted green and the smaller inner nucleus area is tinted red.

task_completion.description: Annotate the composite with a white line or arrow connecting the centroids and flatten the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible white line or arrow drawn on the composite image connecting the center of the red nucleus to the center of the green cell body. The image should appear as an RGB flattened image (e.g., window title says 'RGB').

task_completion.description: Save the annotated composite image to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog pointing to '~/Fiji_Data/results/polarity/annotated_cell.png' or a file manager/terminal showing this file was created.

task_completion.description: Save the displacement measurements to a CSV with specific columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet, text editor, or Fiji Results table showing the columns: Cell_X, Cell_Y, Nucleus_X, Nucleus_Y, Displacement_um, and a save action to 'displacement_metrics.csv'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Fiji GUI for image processing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Fiji menus, toolbars, and image windows should be visible in the screenshots, rather than just a terminal running a headless script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually measured the distance on the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The line/arrow tool should be actively used on the Fiji canvas, or the Fiji 'Results' window should display calculated centroid/length measurements.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV was generated from actual measurements, not just typed out with dummy data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the CSV should logically correspond to the coordinates and length of the line drawn on the 696x520 image, rather than arbitrary or perfectly round numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: BBBC005 (synthetic cells). Images are 696x520 pixels, 8-bit grayscale TIFs. 'w1' images represent the cell body, 'w2' represent the nucleus. The task requires setting the scale to 0.65 µm/pixel (this scale is specific to the prompt and not native to the image metadata). The composite must have w1 (cell body) as green and w2 (nucleus) as red. The final CSV must have exactly these columns: Cell_X, Cell_Y, Nucleus_X, Nucleus_Y, Displacement_um. The measurement accuracy has a 15% tolerance.
